reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully matches the informal claim. A "group of order p²" is encoded as `[Group G] [Fintype G]` with `card G = p^2`, which is equivalent (any group of order p² is automatically finite). "p a prime" is `Nat.Prime p`. The conclusion `∃ N, card N = p ∧ N.Normal` using Mathlib's standard `Subgroup.Normal` exactly expresses "has a normal subgroup of order p". The existentially-bound `Fintype N` instance is trivially satisfiable since any subgroup of a finite group is finite, so it does not change the logical content. No hypotheses are silently added or dropped, and there is no change in quantifier structure or types.
